items.mistake: Patching where the dependency is *defined* instead of where it's *used*.
items.fix: Patch the name as the module-under-test sees it.
items.why: `from openai import OpenAI` in `summarizer.py` creates a local binding `summarizer.OpenAI`; patching `openai.OpenAI` doesn't affect it.

items.mistake: Using session/module scope for fixtures that hold mutable state.
items.fix: Default to `function` scope unless the fixture is provably immutable AND expensive.
items.why: Tests mutate the shared object; later tests see modifications from earlier ones → flakiness.

items.mistake: Mocks that return literal-shape JSON, not the real client's return type.
items.fix: Mirror the real return type (dataclass, pydantic model) — use the real classes constructed manually.
items.why: Tests pass because `.choices[0].message.content` is mocked, but the real client returns objects with different attribute paths or types.
items.example: Better: build a real `ChatCompletion(...)` instance for the test response; the test breaks when the API changes shape.

items.fix: Assert on the outcome that matters to the caller.
items.why: Testing the *conversation* between objects couples the test to internals; harmless refactors break the test.
items.example: Don't assert every helper was called; assert that the final returned summary matches expectations.

items.mistake: Forgetting to use `tmp_path` and writing test artifacts into the repo.
items.fix: Always use `tmp_path` / `tmp_path_factory` for any test that writes files.
items.why: Otherwise tests pollute the working tree, leak between runs, and break on parallel CI workers.
